saints.en: Saturday after the Nativity. Synaxis of the Most Holy Theotokos. Hieromartyr Euthymius, Bishop of Sardis

rubrics:
Вечірня: катизми нема. На «Господи, взиваю» - 6 стихир свята, Сл., І нині: свята (гл. 6, «Слава в вишніх Богу»). Вхід. Великий прокімен «Хто Бог великий» і єктенія «Промовмо всі». Стиховня свята, Сл.: І нині: свята. По «Отче наш» - тропар свята (1). Відпуст великий свята з помин. Собору Пр. Богородиці і свщнмч. Євтимія.
Утреня: по “Бог Господь” – тропар свята (2), Сл., І нині: свята. Катизми чергові і сідал. свята. Пс. 50. Стихира. Канонів два свята. Катавасія: “Христос народжується”. По 3-ій – конд.-ікос та іпакой свята.; по 6-ій – конд.-ікос Собору Пр. Богородиці. Замість “Чеснішу” ірмос канону свята з приспівом. Світил. свята. На Хвалитних – 4 стихири свята, Сл.: Собору, І нині: свята. По вел. славослов'ї – тропар свята (1). Відпуст великий свята з помин. Собору Пр. Богородиці і свщнмч. Євтимія.
Часи: на всіх – тропар свята і конд. собору Богородиці.
Літургія: два перші антифони – свята, третій – загальний з приспівом свята; “Прийдіте, поклонімся” з приспівом свята Тропар свята, Сл., І нині: конд. Собору. Трисвяте. Прокімен, Ап. і Єв. Собору. Суботи по Різдві: Ап. – 1 Тим. 288 зач.; 6, 11 – 16. Єв. – Мт. 46 зач.; 12, 15 – 21. Собору: Ап. – Євр. 306 зач.; 2, 11 – 18. Єв. – Мт. 4 зач.; 2, 13 – 23. Замість «Достойно» – ірмос 9-ї пісні першого святкового канону з приспівом. Причасний свята.

troparion_en.text: O Most Pure Mother of God, your holy Synaxis is adorned with many diverse splendors;  / and gifts are brought to you by many peoples of the world, O Lady Theotokos. /  In your mercy, loosen the bonds of our sinfulness, and save our souls.

kontakion_en.text:
He Who was begotten before the morning star by the Father without a mother  / is incarnate of you today on earth without a father.  / Therefore, a star announces the glad tidings to the Magi, /  while Angels and shepherds sing of your undefiled child-bearing, O you who are full of grace.

(In some Greek sources this Kontakion is chanted in Tone 8, and is listed as the Original Melody.)

matched_oca_saint: Synaxis of the Most Holy Mother of God

oca_saints.name: Second Day of the Nativity of our Lord

troparion.text: Your Nativity, O Christ our God,  /  has shone to the world the Light of knowledge;  /  for by it, those who worshipped the stars / were taught by a star to adore You,  / the Sun of Righteousness,1  /  and to know You, the Dayspring2 from on High.  / O Lord, glory to You!

kontakion.text:
(Original Melody)
Today the Virgin gives birth to the Transcendent One,  /  and the earth offers a cave to the Unapproachable One!  / Angels with shepherds glorify Him.  /  The wise men journey with a star,  /  since for our sake the Pre-Eternal God was born as a young Child.3

oca_saints.name: Synaxis of the Most Holy Mother of God

troparion.text: O Most Pure Mother of God, your holy Synaxis is adorned with many diverse splendors;  / and gifts are brought to you by many peoples of the world, O Lady Theotokos. /  In your mercy, loosen the bonds of our sinfulness, and save our souls.

kontakion.text:
He Who was begotten before the morning star by the Father without a mother  / is incarnate of you today on earth without a father.  / Therefore, a star announces the glad tidings to the Magi, /  while Angels and shepherds sing of your undefiled child-bearing, O you who are full of grace.

(In some Greek sources this Kontakion is chanted in Tone 8, and is listed as the Original Melody.)

troparion.text: By sharing in the ways of the Apostles, / you became a successor to their throne. / Through the practice of virtue, you found the way to divine contemplation, O inspired one of God; / by teaching the word of truth without error, you defended the Faith, / even to the shedding of your blood. / Hieromartyr Euthymius, entreat Christ God to save our souls.

By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Constantine, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

oca_saints.name: Venerable Evarestus of the Studion Monastery

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Evarestus, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

oca_saints.name: Saint Νikodemos the Sanctified of Tismana, Romania

troparion.text: You followed the greatest Shepherd, Who came forth from the Virgin,  / like a sheep of the flock, O Νικόdēmos our Father.  / Through fasting, vigil and prayer, you made yourself a laborer sanctified by heavenly things,  / healing the souls of those who have recourse to you with faith. /  Therefore, we cry: "Glory to Him Who glorified you.  / Glory to Him Who gave you strength. /  Glory to Him Who, through you, grants healing for all."

oca_saints.name: Hieromartyr Constantine the Russian of Lavra on Mount Athos

troparion.text: By sharing in the ways of the Apostles, / you became a successor to their throne. / Through the practice of virtue, you found the way to divine contemplation, O inspired one of God; / by teaching the word of truth without error, you defended the Faith, / even to the shedding of your blood. / Hieromartyr Constantine, entreat Christ God to save our souls.

By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Isaac, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!
